cards.back: inline > id > class/attr/pseudo-class > element; ties go to the later rule.
Width/height include padding and border, so elements stay the size you set.
Flexbox is one-dimensional (row or column); Grid is two-dimensional.
It only applies within a stacking context; a parent context can trap a child.
em is relative to the element's font-size (compounds); rem to the root.
relative offsets from its spot; absolute is placed vs the nearest positioned ancestor.
On the parent: display:flex; align-items:center; justify-content:center.
Adjacent vertical margins merge into the larger one.
It overrides specificity and is hard to undo, causing escalation. Fix specificity instead.
@media (max-width:600px){...} applies styles by screen size — responsive basics.
block stacks full-width; inline flows in text (no width/height); inline-block flows but sizes.
:hover, :focus, :first-child — style an element in a particular state or position.
::before / ::after insert generated content; need a content property.
Animates a property change smoothly over time: transition: opacity .2s.
Move/scale/rotate without affecting layout — and it's GPU-accelerated, so it's smooth.
Shorthand for grow:1, shrink:1, basis:0 — the item takes available space evenly.
Adds space between flex/grid items without margins on each child.
What happens to content that exceeds a box: visible, hidden, scroll, or auto.
% is relative to the parent; vw/vh are relative to the viewport width/height.
Variables: --gap: 8px; then var(--gap) — reusable, themeable values.
> direct child; + next sibling; ~ any following sibling.
Mostly text ones (color, font) inherit; box ones (margin, border) don't.
Use rgba()/#RRGGBBAA or opacity; opacity fades the whole element + children.
